arguments.task: Open official Freshdesk developer and pricing documentation pages and extract evidence for: product description, all documented API authentication methods, API surface type, rate limits, and plan/pricing access needed to use the API. Prefer canonical first-party URLs that load in browser. Read the pages and return concise findings with the exact supporting URLs visited. Also check whether there is any official MCP/server mention on first-party docs.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Freshdesk docs and pricing pages.

raw_result.description: Customer support and helpdesk software with ticketing and omnichannel support features.
raw_result.auth_notes: The Freshdesk API docs explicitly document API key authentication using either HTTP Basic auth with the API key as username and a dummy password, or a Bearer token using a Freshdesk API key. OAuth 2.0 is also documented for apps via Freshworks developer authentication docs, but the gathered evidence in this pass is incomplete on exact Freshdesk app/API scope details.
raw_result.gating_reason: Freshdesk exposes public developer API documentation on a self-serve basis from its official developer site, and public pricing pages are available without sales contact in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API documentation covers core helpdesk resources and actions such as tickets, contacts, companies, conversations, agents, groups, time entries, products, and related support operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official first-party MCP/server mention was confirmed in the gathered evidence.

raw_result.blocker: Authentication details appear buildable from public docs, but this pass did not gather sufficiently complete first-party pricing/access evidence to confirm whether all needed API capabilities are available on a free self-serve plan versus paid tiers.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Freshdesk API docs mention rate limiting, but this corrected pass did not capture the exact limit text strongly enough to restate it verbatim.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer documentation is public and appears broad enough for a first toolkit; auth documentation indicates multiple supported methods rather than OAuth-only.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields still lack fully supported evidence from the pages actually captured in this pass: access_tier, buildability_verdict, and possibly auth_methods/oauth2 scope are not conclusively supported by the gathered official page text alone. Per instruction, uncertain structured fields are marked for human review rather than researching further.
